message.reasoning:
We need to produce JSON with kind, assistant_message, tool_call, acquired_fact_ids, readiness_state.

We are in process of gathering evidence before editing schema definitions. The active code window shows artifacts.rs. The history indicates expansions already done. The frontier suggests expand_file [F1] priority=112 candidates=17.

We need to perform a tool call. Likely session.expand_file with target selector for artifacts.rs. The selector likely is something like "crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs". Use exact short id? The short ids are like F1, S1 etc. The frontier says expand_file [F1] relation=- direction=- candidates=17 priority=112 via session.expand_file. So we should call session.expand_file with target selector maybe "F1"? Actually F1 is the frontier node? The frontier node is the file itself? The graph node for file is maybe a directory node? The short ids: The file artifacts.rs maybe has an id like "F1"? In history they used "target" placeholder. The tool call earlier used "session.expand_file {status:unknown,path:,line_count:0,match_count:0,fields:[target_selector]}". So we need to provide payload with target selector. Probably use the short id of the file, maybe "F1"? The frontier says "[F1] relation=- direction=- candidates=17 priority=112 via session.expand_file". So the node id is F1. So we should call session.expand_file with target_selector set to "F1". Also maybe include depth etc? Minimal payload: {"target":"F1"}? The contract: session.expand_file payload includes target selector and optional depth, limit, etc. So we can just provide target. So tool_call name "session.expand_file". Payload: {"target":"F1"}.

We also need acquired_fact_ids: list of fact ids we are acquiring? Probably we can include something like ["F1"]? Or maybe we capture something else. The instructions: "acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"], list of fact ids acquired in this turn. We might not have any yet; maybe we can leave empty array. But maybe we want to include the fact id of the expanded file? Could be "F1". I'd include ["F1"].

Readiness_state: we are not ready yet, maybe "evidence_acquired"? Actually we are expanding to gather evidence, so we have evidence visible? The states: "not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit". We have expanded to gather evidence, so evidence_visible? Or evidence_acquired? Probably "evidence_acquired" after expansion. But we haven't yet retrieved; we are about to call expand_file. So maybe readiness_state is "evidence_visible"? Hmm.

The response contract: When kind is tool_call, we must include tool_call and other fields. assistant_message is a brief status. So something like "Expanding artifacts.rs file to capture schema version symbols". tool_call includes tool_name and payload. acquired_fact_ids list of fact ids acquired. readiness_state "evidence_visible"? I'd choose "evidence_visible" since we are making it visible.

Thus JSON:

{
  "kind":"tool_call",
  "assistant_message":"Expanding artifacts.rs file to capture schema version symbols",
  "tool_call":{"tool_name":"session.expand_file","payload":{"target":"F1"}},
  "acquired_fact_ids":["F1"],
  "readiness_state":"evidence_visible"
}

Make sure no extra fields. Provide JSON only.
